matcher mode `ml` with config source reused from state; evaluated pairs: dataset_names, dataset_signature, fusion_size_estimate, id_columns, llm_trace_path, matching_strategies, min_blocking_pc_for_matching, proxy_f1_margin, proxy_max_candidates, run_report_path; selected scores: matching_strategies (threshold=0.78, f1=0.7738095238095238, threshold=0.72, f1=0.8962962962962964, f1=0.8831168831168832), fusion_size_estimate (f1=0.7738095238095238, f1=0.8962962962962964, f1=0.8831168831168832); produced 10 pair strategy entries.
This run was an initial draft of the pipeline.
The pipeline was constructed to include embedding-based blocking using the 'sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2' model with top_k=15 for candidate selection.
ML-based matching was implemented using feature extractors with string, numeric, and date comparators tailored for each dataset pair.
Grid search with cross-validation was used to select the best classifier among RandomForest, LogisticRegression, GradientBoosting, and SVM for each dataset pair.
Data fusion strategies were added for 12 attributes, predominantly using 'longest_string' for 6 attributes, 'median' for 5 numeric/date attributes, and 'union' for the 'genres' attribute.
The fusion strategy emphasizes 'longest_string' as the dominant method for string attributes, with 'median' used for numeric and date attributes, and 'union' for set-like attributes.
The pipeline includes embedding-based blocking to improve candidate selection before matching.
Singleton records are included in the fusion process, and lineage fields '_id' and 'source_id' are used for alignment.
List-like columns in datasets are detected and normalized before processing.
pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 1; fusion_data.csv was produced.

The current overall accuracy is 67.86%, with a macro accuracy of 67.86% based on 20 evaluated records and 7 evaluated attributes.

All 13 transformed columns were processed using the 'strip + normalize_whitespace' transformation.
List-format inconsistencies in 'author' and 'genres' columns were addressed by normalizing to a consistent list-aware encoding and flattening stringified list artifacts.
Author role annotations were stripped to canonicalize author text.
Text columns 'title', 'publisher', and 'language' were canonicalized by stripping and normalizing whitespace while preserving validation-style casing.
Language column was kept scalar as multi-valued fused strings were canonicalized to a primary value.
No country normalization was applied as no mismatch evidence was found.

matcher mode `ml` with config source reused from state; evaluated pairs: dataset_names, dataset_signature, fusion_size_estimate, id_columns, llm_trace_path, matching_strategies, min_blocking_pc_for_matching, proxy_f1_margin, proxy_max_candidates, run_report_path; selected scores: matching_strategies (threshold=0.78, f1=0.7738095238095238, threshold=0.72, f1=0.8962962962962964, f1=0.8831168831168832), fusion_size_estimate (f1=0.7738095238095238, f1=0.8962962962962964, f1=0.8831168831168832); produced 10 pair strategy entries.
This run is a refinement of the existing pipeline based on evaluation feedback.
Replaced generic longest_string fusion for author, title, publisher, and language with attribute-specific canonical fusion functions wrapped in a safe fuser.
Replaced median fusion for numeric attributes like publish_year, page_count, rating, numratings, price with a source-aware prefer_higher_trust fusion strategy or attribute-specific safe fusers.
Changed fusion for genres, bookformat, and edition to prefer_higher_trust instead of union or longest_string.
Added preprocessing steps to normalize and clean author, title, publisher, and language fields before matching and fusion.
Enabled singleton retention and included lineage fields (_id, source_id, source_dataset) in the pipeline.
Fusion behavior shifted from using generic longest_string and median fusers to source-aware, attribute-specific fusion functions that prefer trusted sources and perform canonical normalization.

The pipeline now prefers values from the most trusted source (metabooks_small) when available, falling back to goodreads and then amazon, improving fusion accuracy and reducing noise from verbose or noisy strings.

The overall accuracy on the evaluation set was approximately 61.4% with 20 records and 7 attributes evaluated.
Key metric signals included low accuracy for genres (5%), publisher (35%), page_count (65%), author (40%), and title (60%), indicating weak fusion logic or normalization mismatches.
Coverage of gold IDs was incomplete with only 85% mapped coverage and 5% direct coverage, indicating some reference entities were missing in the fused output.
High severity issues included low attribute accuracy for author (40%) and list-format inconsistencies detected in 19 cases, especially for genres.
Medium severity issues included incomplete evaluation gold ID coverage, low accuracy for page_count (65%), publisher (60%), and title (60%).
Normalization succeeded on attempt 3 and produced 3 normalized dataset files.
All 13 transformed columns were processed using strip and normalize_whitespace transformations.
List normalization was applied to the author and genres columns to address inconsistencies such as list/scalar/stringified-list mismatches and multi-contributor strings with role suffixes.
Scalar text canonicalization was applied to language, publisher, and title columns to handle multi-valued fused strings, abbreviations, punctuation, and whitespace/token differences.
No country normalization or blanket lowercasing was applied due to lack of evidence supporting these transformations.
The dominant transform pattern was 'strip + normalize_whitespace' applied across all 13 transformed columns in the three datasets: amazon_small, goodreads_small, and metabooks_small.

matcher mode `ml` with config source reused from state; evaluated pairs: dataset_names, dataset_signature, fusion_size_estimate, id_columns, llm_trace_path, matching_strategies, min_blocking_pc_for_matching, proxy_f1_margin, proxy_max_candidates, run_report_path; selected scores: matching_strategies (threshold=0.78, f1=0.7738095238095238, threshold=0.72, f1=0.8962962962962964, f1=0.8831168831168832), fusion_size_estimate (f1=0.7738095238095238, f1=0.8962962962962964, f1=0.8831168831168832); produced 10 pair strategy entries.
This run was an evaluation refinement of the existing pipeline.
The fusion strategy for the 'genres' attribute was changed from 'prefer_higher_trust' to a custom safe fuser '_pydi_safe_fuser(genres_fuser)'.
The pipeline now uses custom safe fusers for several attributes including title, author, publish_year, publisher, language, genres, and page_count.
The fusion logic was adjusted to improve attribute-level reconciliation, especially for genres and publisher.
Singleton retention was enabled in the pipeline.
The fusion behavior shifted from a simplistic source-preference approach to more robust, attribute-specific fusion functions that handle list-like attributes and consensus-based numeric fusion.
Genres fusion moved from selecting the highest-trust source's short list to a union-style enrichment that parses list-like values, unions tokens across sources, and deduplicates case-insensitively.
Publisher fusion was adjusted to prefer richer canonical values rather than shortest strings, using source trust only as a tie-breaker.
Author fusion now explicitly prefers the metabooks_small source and applies better contributor cleaning.

output_summary: This run was a repair of the pipeline after a previous execution failure.
The fusion logic was reworked to mainly use the prefer_higher_trust fuser across five attributes.
Custom safe fusers were applied for author, genres, language, title, publish_year, publisher, and page_count attributes.
Singleton retention was enabled in the pipeline.
Lineage fields _id, source_id, and source_dataset were included for alignment.

The fusion strategy shifted from simplistic source-preference fusion that favored shortest strings and highest-trust values without reconciliation to a more nuanced approach using custom safe fusers for complex attributes.

The pipeline now uses prefer_higher_trust as the dominant fusion strategy but supplements it with specialized fusers for author, genres, language, and numeric fields to improve attribute-level reconciliation.
Normalization and cleaning functions for author, publisher, genres, language, and title were applied before fusion to ensure consistent input.
List-like columns were detected and normalized prior to fusion to handle list/scalar inconsistencies.
Blocking was performed using embedding-based blockers with text columns prepared for blocking by cleaning and normalization.

Key metric signals include low accuracies for genres (0.05), publisher (0.35), and page_count (0.65), while language accuracy is relatively high at 0.85.
